files.rowids_format: uint32_t[row_count] grouped by adsh_code in input row order

storage_extensions.usage: mmap offsets.bin and rowids.bin; iterate only the `num` rowid slices for FY-2023 filing codes, so the hot loop skips non-qualifying filings and does not read `num.adsh` per row

join_order.notes: Apply the selective filing filter first, keep a dense name lookup keyed by adsh code, and drive all downstream work only from qualifying filings.

join_order.build: tiny per-filing hash_multimap(tag,version -> plabel rows) from pre_is_by_adsh(qualifying adsh only)
join_order.probe: num rows for the same filing from num.adsh.postings_dense after uom='USD' and value IS NOT NULL
join_order.notes: Rebuild a cache-resident exact map per filing and probe it once per qualifying num row, avoiding repeated linear scans over the same pre slice.

data_structures.fact_join: flat_array(pre_is_offsets_by_adsh) + compact SoA payload arrays for qualifying stmt='IS' rows + tiny per-filing hash_multimap keyed by (tag,version)

optimization_notes: Root cause: the iter_3 plan still does Category A/C work that is disproportionate to the selective filing filter, scanning 33.2M USD posting rows and rescanning each qualifying filing's roughly 20.5-row `pre` slice for every surviving `num` row, which yields about 210.7M tag/version comparisons on average. The revised plan switches to a filing-centric execution path: filter `sub` first, keep only the 26,616 qualifying adsh codes, build `pre` payloads only for those filings, and use the existing `num.adsh.postings_dense` storage extension to scan only about 12.2M `num` rows from qualifying filings. Within each filing, build a tiny cache-resident `(tag,version)` multimap over the filtered `pre` rows and probe it once per qualifying `num` row, eliminating repeated scans of the same `pre` slice and removing `num.adsh` loads from the hot loop.
